tagline: Yesterday's good news, bright and early.

stories.headline: Young Champion Erases 10-Stroke Deficit for Historic Major Victory
stories.summary: Twenty-five-year-old Yoo Hae-ran erases a 10-stroke deficit to claim her first LPGA major at Hazeltine, winning $1.95 million and stopping world No. 1 Nelly Korda's three-peat bid.

stories.headline: Ranked 499th a Year Ago, Columbia Student Stuns at Wimbledon
stories.summary: Former Columbia University student Michael Zheng defeats a Grand Slam semifinalist in his first Wimbledon main draw match on grass.

stories.headline: Injury-Ravaged New Zealand Beats the Odds to Win in England
stories.summary: Injury-ravaged New Zealand's underdogs claim their first Test series victory in England in 40 years, bound by brotherhood against the odds.

stories.headline: 60,000 Oysters Return Home to Scottish Waters After a Century
stories.summary: Communities doubled their goal, restoring over 60,000 European flat oysters to the Firth of Forth after a hundred years of absence.

stories.headline: 175 Volunteers Build 102 Bicycles for Displaced Maui Children
stories.summary: About 100 volunteers at Crafting for Communities assembled 102 bicycles for Maui's displaced children and foster youth.

stories.headline: Thai Qualifier Saves Match Point and Claims Grand Slam Breakthrough
stories.summary: Ranked No. 164 globally, 23-year-old Thai qualifier Mananchaya Sawangkaew saves one match point to defeat 20th seed Maja Chwalinska and claim her first Grand Slam victory.

stories.headline: Juno the Leatherback Turtle Returns to Nest After 25 Years of Devoted Stewardship
stories.summary: Beloved scarred sea turtle Juno, tracked for 25 years, returns to lay her 300th nest of the season, a triumph for Florida conservation.

stories.headline: Breakthrough Drug Restores Movement in Huntington's Disease
stories.summary: The experimental drug anle138b reduced toxic protein clumps in treated mice's brains and extended their lifespan, offering hope for Huntington's disease patients.

stories.headline: Family Feeds Neighbors Night After Night, With Love as the Main Ingredient
stories.summary: Ashlee Graves and her family cook and serve free hot meals to neighbors several days a week, sustained by donations and their own hearts.

stories.headline: Community Crafters Stitch 5,000 Comfort Items Monthly for Hospitals and Hospices
stories.summary: About 100 volunteers at Crafting for Communities hand-stitch roughly 5,000 comfort items monthly—from dementia dolls to sensory toys—for hospitals and hospices across the Midlands.

stories.headline: World's Last Wild Tree Species Gets a Second Chance at Life
stories.summary: Seeds from the planet's last wild Dendroseris neriifolia tree arrived at Kew in April 2026, with 25 of 29 seeds viable and eight seedlings now establishing hope against extinction.

stories.headline: Stranger Drives Hours to Deliver Pizza, Cash, and Hope to Cancer Family
stories.summary: Shawn Rizk drove 12 hours across Ontario with a pickup truck overflowing with community donations—pizza, cash, furniture, and care packages—to help the Reed family as Chris battles rare stage four cancer.

stories.headline: Gold Nanoparticles Could End Surgery Cycles for Millions with Endometriosis
stories.summary: Scientists developed gold-laced nanoparticles that hitchhike on white blood cells to diagnose and treat endometriosis without repeated surgeries, sparing millions of women ongoing pain.

stories.headline: Rally Legend Orchestrates Dramatic Comeback for 69th Career Victory
stories.summary: Sébastien Ogier seizes his moment to capture his 69th WRC career victory at Greece's Acropolis Rally, returning to the top for the first time in 15 years since 2011.

stories.headline: Table Sugar Could Transform How We Make Life-Saving Medicines
stories.summary: Scientists discover a one-step method using table sugar and vinegar to create vital diabetes drugs at a fraction of current costs, potentially transforming affordable medicine production.

stories.headline: 3D-Printed Battery Seven Times Stronger Powers Clean Energy Future
stories.summary: UCLA researchers created a 3D-printed zinc-ion hybrid battery storing more than seven times the charge of similar devices, offering cheaper renewable energy storage since zinc is 100 times more abundant than lithium.
